items.title: A Liberal-Green government. Could it happen in Australia?

children.content: In many ways, the Liberals and the Greens are complete opposites — but in the ACT, an Australian-first power-sharing arrangement between the two parties almost became a reality. So, in politics, can opposites really attract?

image.altText: A graphic of a hand placing a vote with a half blue half green background.

items.title: 'Inhumane': Mick's disgust at cops' justification over the tasering of his 95yo mum

children.content: In an in-depth interview ahead of the inquest into his mother Clare Nowland's nursing home death, Mick says the family wants changes that will prevent another tragedy.

image.altText: An older man wearing a short-sleeve black button up shirt stands in his backyard in front of a green fence and hedges.

items.title: Motorcyclist's death a reminder to pay attention when driving, loved ones say

children.content: The loved ones of a Canberra motorcyclist killed nearly two years ago say his death is a stark reminder that "full attention" behind the wheel can be the difference between life and death.

gallery.altText: A bald man with glasses and a white beard stands outdoors smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A bald man with glasses and a white beard stands outdoors smiling.

items.title: Truck driver found guilty of negligent driving over fatal crash in Canberra

children.content: Sean Joshua Walton was driving his truck and turning right at an intersection when he crashed into a black Harley Davidson motorbike being driven by 47-year-old Craig O'Neil.

image.altText: A crumpled motorbike on the ground near a parked truck, at night at an intersection.

items.title: 'Obvious I lied': Former Canberra doctor faces disciplinary action for lying under oath

children.content: Former Canberra vascular registrar Imran Kader will face disciplinary action after he lied under oath and tried to interfere with a witness during criminal proceedings for an alleged rape.

image.altText: A building sign that reads 'ACT Civil and Administrative Tribunal'.

items.title: Outgoing chief magistrate on mistakes, rehabilitation and compassion

children.content: Heart is not something anyone expects from the court system, but it was something outgoing ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker, who retired in March after 15 years in the job, aimed to portray.

items.title: 'Breach of trust': Woman convicted of illegally obtaining medication

children.content: A Canberra speech pathologist is convicted of using the details of people she knew, including parents of two of her patients, to illegally obtain prescription medication.

image.altText: A concrete building reading "Law Courts of the Australian Capital Territory", with Australian, ACT and Aboriginal flag.

items.title: Hopes new Canberra strata recommendations will dispel 'shonks' from industry

children.content: An ACT Legislative Assembly committee inquiry wants to see strata managers get mandatory licenses.

image.altText: From the ground up, a large brown brick apartment complex stands, with a cloudy blue sky backdrop.

items.title: Police charge man with murdering five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs

children.content: NT police charge Jefferson Lewis with murdering the girl, who disappeared from the Old Timers/Ilyperenye town camp last weekend.

articles.title: Police release CCTV of 'outright looting' of Alice Springs businesses

children.content: NT Police have released CCTV footage of the "outright looting" of businesses caught up in rioting following the arrest of Jefferson Lewis for the murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

image.altText: A large group of people swarms into a service station

items.title: Penrith survives sin-binning and controversial call to scrape past Manly

children.content: The ladder-leading Panthers are pushed all the way, but cling on to notch a hard-fought 18-16 victory over the Sea Eagles. Follow live.

image.altText: Two rugby league players, one in white and one in black, tussle

items.title: Suns pegged back after fast start as King flattened in marking contest

children.content: The GWS Giants burst to life after a slow start at Carrara Stadium, as Ben King is left worse for wear after a big marking contest. Follow live.

image.altText: An AFL player in red receives treatment from medical staff while on the pitch

items.title: Sister of deceased rock climber Angela Ha says the best way to honour her is to 'live life to the fullest'

children.content: Michelle Ha's older sister Angela died after falling from the Organ Pipes while rock climbing at Arapiles in western Victoria last week.

items.title: By-election of no consequence leaves some major party insiders disturbed

children.content: The Nepean by-election proves Victoria's election in November is likely to be tough for the two major parties, Richard Willingham writes.

avatar.alt: Richard Willingham in front of a white background, stares straight down the barrel.

items.title: A year after the landslide, Albanese faces a harsher reality

children.content: The prime minister's second term is now defined less by what he wants to do than by what unforeseen events will allow.

image.altText: anthony albanese holds up medicare card

items.title: Nomadic family says 'dream is more powerful' than surging fuel prices

children.content: While many Australians are thinking twice about big road trips due to fuel costs, the Sharavnyam family from Mongolia is determined to fulfil a dream to travel the world.

items.title: Military plane makes emergency landing at outback airport

children.content: The C-27J Spartan aircraft took off from Darwin, and the ABC understood it was heading towards Brisbane and near Longreach when personnel observed "signs of smoke" onboard.

image.altText: A large military plane parked on runway with an ambulance next to it.

items.title: The cafe meeting, the $2m bounty and the botched hit that shocked Sydney

children.content: As the house of Hamzy began to fall one of its senior members hatched a million-dollar plan to take out rivals in revenge. What followed has been a secret for five years.

image.altText: Ibrahem Hamze 2

items.title: Love story: An accident changed their lives but strengthened their bond

children.content: The goalposts moved dramatically on Beau and Lucy Vernon's life plans, but it brought them closer together.

items.title: A 17-year-old Go-Betweens song inspired Robert Forster's debut novel

children.content: The Go-Betweens frontman spent years developing the story, which follows a pair of musicians on the run from the law as they try to clear their names.

items.title: 'Soundtrack to Australia': ARIA reveals six 2026 Hall of Fame inductees

children.content: For the first time in more than a decade, multiple artists are being inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame.

items.title: What this truck full of fibreglass sheets tells us about building price pressures

children.content: On Australia's busiest freight route, it takes four shots of coffee, $2,553 of fuel and a little hair metal to keep the wheels of the economy turning.

items.title: Trump reviews Iran peace proposal as speculation turns to strikes resuming — as it happened

children.content: The US president told reporters he was reviewing a new peace proposal from Iranian negotiators, but immediately cast doubt on whether it would be "acceptable".

image.altText: Donald Trump leans towards a group of reporters in front of the presidential plane Air Force One.

items.title: Firefighters who died battling blaze, from cancer added to honour roll

children.content: Hundreds of people have honoured the families and legacies of Western Australia's fallen firefighters at a memorial service in Kings Park on Sunday.

image.altText: A memorial service is held at the Firefighter's Memorial Grove in Kings Park.

items.title: 'I made the choice': Why James Valentine resolved to die on his own terms

children.content: Broadcaster James Valentine was offered two different treatments for his cancer, so he chose the one that let him keep doing what he loved. Australian Story followed Valentine over his final 12 months as he faced the end with courage, purpose and humour.

items.title: 'If the cops came, you'd slip out the back': The shifting role of Aussie pubs

children.content: From a tiny Riverina pub to a polished inner-city institution, Australia's local pubs are changing hands at record pace. As big money pours in, what happens to the communities that built them?

items.title: Government announces program to unlock land for oil infrastructure

children.content: The state government has announced the Accelerating Fuel Infrastructure Program, which it says is intended to ease fuel costs and protect against future price shocks.

items.title: Aussies put Olympic heartbreak to bed with historic world title

children.content: Australia, despite not having a dedicated curling facility, has claimed a first ever world championship title, with Tahli Gill and Dean Hewitt victorious in Switzerland overnight.

image.altText: Tahli Gill and Dean Hewitt hug

items.title: Australian says Gaza aid boat activists were beaten by Israeli navy

children.content: Zack Schofield was one of three Australians taken to hospital after being released by Israeli authorities, and says he saw at least 10 of his crew being shot by rubber bullets.

image.altText: A young man in a light brown jumper speaks to a camera he's holding up in a kitchenette.

items.title: 'Disgusted': Survivors speak out against video games depicting scam centres

children.content: Scam centre survivors say they are hurt by games that serialise scam compound experiences online, but one creator says he hopes people find the game "fun".

items.title: King Charles won over Trump with his visit but not Zohran Mamdani

children.content: King Charles's US state visit hit a bump in New York when Mayor Zohran Mamdani said if the pair were to speak, he would "probably encourage him to return the Koh-i-Noor diamond".

image.altText: King Charles standing next to Queen Camilla leans in to listen to Zohran Mamdani.

items.title: Australian taxpayers take on risk for major oil and gas projects

children.content: Australian governments take on risk to get major resource projects up and running, so why don't they take an equity stake?

items.title: Dancing queens return to Trundle ABBA Festival to celebrate pop icons

children.content: For one day each year, the town of 335 people swaps tractors for tribute acts, grain trucks for glitter and everyday life for an all-out ABBA extravaganza.
